data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Netflix uses a microservices architecture to split its application into smaller, independent services.
data.technical_spec: Each microservice is responsible for a single feature or functionality and communicates via APIs.

data.title: Cloud-Based Infrastructure
data.description: Relies heavily on Amazon Web Services (AWS) for scalable cloud computing resources.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes AWS EC2 instances and S3 buckets for storage and computing.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: Netflix employs a global CDN to deliver content efficiently to users worldwide.
data.technical_spec: Uses Netflix Open Connect alongside commercial CDNs.

data.title: NoSQL Databases
data.description: Netflix uses NoSQL databases such as Cassandra for handling large volumes of user data.
data.technical_spec: Cassandra is used for its ability to handle massive amounts of streaming data.

data.title: Event-Driven Architecture
data.description: Utilizes an event-driven architecture to process user actions and system events asynchronously.
data.technical_spec: Kafka is used for message brokering and real-time data streaming.
